nodes.label: O.D.I.N. (Optical Detection & Intelligence Network)
nodes.rationale: Local-first personal assistant; UI themed after tree-of-life mockups in design/ with an animated Odin head whose eyes and aura react to live speech amplitude.

nodes.label: Ollama Local LLM Provider
nodes.rationale: Default local LLM provider at http://127.0.0.1:11434. When OLLAMA_MODEL is unset, Jarvis uses the last model loaded from Settings, otherwise auto-selects the first installed chat model, deliberately skipping embedding-only models like nomic-embed-text.

nodes.label: Semantic Long-Term Memory (vectors.db)
nodes.rationale: On by default: every message and memory document is embedded through Ollama's nomic-embed-text into data/vectors.db and recalled by meaning across conversations. Configurable via JARVIS_EMBED_MODEL, JARVIS_VECTOR_DB_PATH, JARVIS_VECTOR_PROVIDER=disabled.

nodes.label: Core Memory Blocks (Persona + User Profile)
nodes.rationale: Odin's persona and a profile of the user are always included in the prompt and editable under Data Map → Core Memory, guaranteeing identity continuity regardless of vector recall.

nodes.label: Sleep-Time Memory Consolidation
nodes.rationale: Daily job at 4:30 AM (or on demand via POST /api/v1/memory/consolidate) distills recent conversations into durable facts and refreshes the user profile; tunable via JARVIS_CONSOLIDATION_HOUR or JARVIS_CONSOLIDATION=disabled.

nodes.label: ChromaDB Alternative Vector Backend
nodes.rationale: Optional vector backend installed with the [vector] extra and pointed at JARVIS_CHROMA_PATH; kept available even though the default vector store is the built-in vectors.db.

nodes.label: Encrypted Full-State Backup & Restore
nodes.rationale: AES-GCM authenticated encryption covers SQLite, settings, audit logs, and Chroma data. The key (data/backup.key or JARVIS_BACKUP_KEY) is never included in a backup. Restore validates the bundle and SQLite integrity, coordinates with live DB activity, and creates an encrypted safety backup before replacement. Daily backup at 4:00 AM, latest 30 retained, with catch-up backup if the daily run was missed.

nodes.label: Piper Neural Voice (TTS)
nodes.rationale: Used when the [voice] extra is installed and a model exists at ~/jarvis-models/piper/en_US-ryan-medium.onnx (JARVIS_PIPER_VOICE override); deliberately falls back to macOS `say` so speech always works.

nodes.label: Wake Word Listener (openwakeword)
nodes.rationale: Optional, enabled in Settings → Voice; ships with hey_jarvis as the default model (JARVIS_WAKE_MODEL override) until a custom 'Hey Odin' model is trained; opens the chat dock when heard.

nodes.label: Local Whisper Speech-to-Text
nodes.rationale: Automatically uses local whisper-cli with ~/jarvis-models/ggml-base.en.bin by default (JARVIS_WHISPER_MODEL override); a guided download exists in Settings → Voice.

nodes.label: Functional Bots (research, code, system, file)
nodes.rationale: Research bot does a bounded DuckDuckGo HTML lookup, code bot analyzes real local files, system bot executes a parsed command without shell expansion, file bot writes files. Invoked via natural-language actions or /<bot> <action> slash commands.

nodes.label: One-Time Permission Approvals & Audit Logging
nodes.rationale: Permissions set to prompt create a pending one-time approval in Settings; approving executes that exact queued action. Writes inside the repo need no prompt, writes outside require one-time write_files approval — bounding risk while keeping in-repo workflows frictionless.

nodes.label: Real-Time System Telemetry (system.metrics)
nodes.rationale: CPU, memory, disk, network, battery, and uptime are streamed over the events WebSocket as system.metrics, plus /api/v1/system/overview exposes live subsystem node statuses that drive the tree-of-life stage.

nodes.label: Electron Desktop Wrapper
nodes.rationale: Wraps the built React app, starts and stops the FastAPI backend automatically, reports startup health for model, voice, memory, and backups, and requests macOS microphone access on first use.

edges.source_location: consolidation distills conversations into durable facts stored as memory
consolidation refreshes the profile core memory block
ChromaDB is the documented alternative to the default vectors.db backend — same vector-memory role, different store
backups include SQLite; restore validates SQLite integrity
encrypted backups include configured Chroma data
wake word opens the chat dock that then uses Whisper speech input
Odin speaks with a local Piper neural voice
bot actions gated by prompt/allowed permissions and one-time approvals
desktop app starts and stops the FastAPI backend automatically
tree-of-life nodes driven by live telemetry
persistent SQLite-backed conversations and messages at data/jarvis.db

hyperedges.label: Memory Subsystem (vector recall, core blocks, consolidation, alternative backend)
